channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: I Can’t Decide Which ‘Resident Evil Requiem’ Perspective I Liked More
item.description: The new game makes you play first person for scares and third person for action, which creates excellent balance.

item.title: Trump’s tariffs face a fresh legal test in federal court
item.description: The tariffs are scheduled to expire July 24. The centerpiece of President Donald Trump‘s economic policy — sweeping taxes on global imports — is under legal assault again.

item.title: ‘Computers are no longer a bicycle for the mind’: Frameworks founder says the Steve Jobs era is over and PCs are now a ‘self-driving car that takes you directly to the destination’
item.description: As Framework prepares for its next big product launch, the repair-and-augment-friendly PC maker has some thoughts about the Cloud and AI revolution.

item.title: Le leasing social revient en juin : la rustine électrique du gouvernement face au prix de l’essence
item.description: Le gouvernement sort enfin du silence. Face à l'envolée des prix à la pompe causée par la crise au Moyen-Orient, Sébastien Lecornu vient de dégainer son plan d'électrification. La mesure phare ? Le retour du leasing social dès juin 2026.

item.title: Motorola’s Souped-Up Folding Phone Is Almost Half Off
item.description: Amazon has the Razr Ultra marked down by $600 for a limited time.

item.title: I Rewatched the Live-Action ‘Speed Racer’ to Feel Something Again
item.description: The Wachowskis' film deserves all the flowers for being ahead of its time as one of the best live-action anime works ever.

item.title: Anthropic’s Mythos Will Force a Cybersecurity Reckoning—Just Not the One You Think
item.description: The new AI model is being heralded—and feared—as a hacker’s superweapon. Experts say its arrival is a wake-up call for developers who have long made security an afterthought.

item.title: The Future of the Artemis Program Is Riding on Reentry
item.description: Entire space programs have been canceled after a failure in the reentry phase. In the final test for Artemis II, astronauts will travel at 32 times the speed of sound as they return from the moon.

item.title: The new MacBook Air has already dropped to a record-low price on Amazon
item.description: Save $150 on Apple's latest M5-powered laptop

item.title: Microsoft Says You Should Take Copilot Seriously but Not Literally
item.description: More than entertainment, less than legally liable.

item.title: Microsoft finally lets Windows 11 testers unlock experimental features without ViVeTool
item.description: Microsoft is finally allowing Windows 11 testers to get access to experimental features without having to download a third-party app like ViVeTool. The changes are part of making the Windows Insider Program (WIP) less confusing, with Dev and Canary testing rings simplified into a new Experimental Channel alongside a refresh of the existing Beta Channel. […]...

item.title: ‘Dune 3’ IMAX movie tickets are selling for thousands of dollars on Ebay
item.description: Hype for the final film in the sci-fi trilogy is sky-high, and ticket resellers are offering ludicrous prices to match. According to director Denis Villeneuve, Dune: Part Three is meant to be watched on a 70-millimeter IMAX screen.

item.title: Building a sharper brain is easier than you think. Here are 5 tips
item.description: Building a better brain is possible. Below, Majid Fotuhi shares five key insights from his new book, The Invincible Brain: The Clinically Proven Plan to Age-Proof Your Brain and Stay Sharp for Life.

item.title: J’ai visité l’usine européenne de Tesla : voici les vrais secrets de fabrication de la Model Y
item.description: J'ai pu franchir les portes de la tentaculaire Gigafactory de Tesla à Berlin, le cœur européen d'Elon Musk. Entre presses titanesques et rythme effréné, on s'attend à une automatisation totale et glaçante. Pourtant, derrière la débauche technologique, la réalité des lignes de montage cache un contraste saisissant. Surtout lorsqu'on revient tout juste des usines robotisées de Xiaomi... et Tesla en Chine. Embarquement pour une visite hors norme. ...

item.title: My go-to electric screwdriver is on sale for over 50 percent off today
item.description: Fanttik, like Hoto, is quickly becoming a household name. The tool brand’s cordless wares are quiet, capable, and certainly more stylish than anything you’d find at your local Ace Hardware or neighborhood tool library. The S1 Pro electric screwdriver is a great example of the company’s penchant for attractive design, one you can currently grab […]

item.title: Excellente affaire sur le marché du milieu de gamme avec l’Oppo Reno 14 FS (OLED, 6 000 mAh, IP69) qui perd 30 % de son prix
item.description: L'Oppo Reno 14 FS est un smartphone paré à toutes les situations grâce à sa certification IP69 assurant son étanchéité. Chez E.Leclerc, il perd presque 150 euros de son prix pour être affiché à 283,73 euros.

item.title: The Trump Administration Wants Gamers to Step Up and Fill the Air Traffic Controller Shortage
item.description: Shockingly, the answer to a long-running and complex issue is not "gamers."

item.title: How influencers fiercely strategize behind the scenes a Coachella
item.description: The constant at Coachella is an insatiable appetite online for any and all festival-related content. Sam Mintesnot had checked off everything she possibly could have from a long list of to-dos in preparation for the Coachella music festival. She crafted the best outfits, got her hair and nails done, booked a one-way ticket to Los Angeles and flew out on Tuesday with a spreadsheet full of ideas for videos she could post related to the festival....

item.title: Watch a Comet Get Torn Apart by the Sun and Become a Headless Ghost
item.description: Comet MAPS (C/2026 A1) didn't survive its close encounter with the Sun, ruining its chances of brightening up the night skies.

item.title: Amazon Luna axes third-party game purchases
item.description: Amazon Luna announced a big change on Friday that will prevent players from purchasing third-party games and subscriptions. The cloud gaming service will remove previously purchased games on June 10th, 2026, though they'll still be available to play on other platforms through the EA, GOG, or Ubisoft accounts players used when purchasing the title. In […]...

item.title: The Iranian Lego AI video creators credit their virality to ‘heart’
item.description: Donald Trump has spun the recent rescue of a downed airman whose fighter jet was destroyed behind Iranian borders as a resounding success. But the story is very different in one of the many viral, AI-generated Lego videos that have been produced by Iranian content creation group Explosive Media in the weeks since the US […]

item.title: The college industry is becoming K-shaped as acceptance rates plummet. What’s happening to admissions?
item.description: Prestigious universities are seeing higher demand than ever, while smaller schools are closing their doors. So, what’s driving the split? It’s a low time for higher education, depending on where you look.

item.title: Your YouTube Premium bill is going up. Here’s the new monthly cost
item.description: The popular subscription now costs $15.99 a month, with family and student plans also increasing. If you pay to keep ads out of your YouTube experience, keep an eye on your monthly bill.

item.title: Sam Altman’s Home Hit With Molotov Cocktail, 20-Year-Old Man Arrested
item.description: He also allegedly went to OpenAI HQ and threatened to burn down the building.

item.title: No, Elon Musk doesn't want to give you a $5,000 tax refund — it's a scam, here's what to look out for
item.description: Elon Musk's name is being used in crypto scams once again.

item.title: Amazon Luna ends support for third-party subscriptions and game purchases
item.description: Amazon is ending support for third-party integrations on its Luna cloud gaming service. The most immediate changes mean that it's no longer possible to buy Ubisoft+ and Jackbox Games subscriptions or standalone games through Luna. Amazon will automatically any cancel active subscriptions bought through Luna at the end of customers' next billing cycle. If you have a Ubisoft+ subscription that you bought directly from Ubisoft instead, you’ll still be able to access games on that service through Luna until June 10.The Bring Your Own Library option — which allows users to play games they own on th...

item.title: The AI Doc director says cynicism is the only wrong answer to AI
item.description: 'The AI Doc' director Daniel Roher talks about Sam Altman’s weird vibes, AI in filmmaking, and the folly of cynicism.
